name: insight-dispersion-verified
summary: Dispersion measured end-to-end: BTL arc BPMs 9-11 (s=179-189) show 14-22 mm orbit shift per unit dW/W vs 1.8 in straights — 12x localization exactly at the bends. The energy-jitter-at-arc-BPMs diagnostic signature is real and usable.

name: insight-calibration-confounded
summary: CAUTION: end-of-linac TOF calibration of a MID-linac cavity gives dW/dV = -0.52 (not +cos(phi_s) = +0.92): downstream phase-slip feedback overcompensates the local energy gain. Calibrate cavities with local TOF pairs or downstream RF considerations, exactly as real machines do (phase-scan with downstream cavities detuned). Only the LAST cavity reads naive.

name: insight-fault-compensation-works
summary: Single-cavity fault compensation validated: swapping 20% amplitude (or +/-6 deg phase) between SSR2:CAV17/18 holds energy within 0.5 MeV, T flat, losses within 15%. Neighbor compensation is a viable recovery recipe for cavity faults.

name: insight-blm-nonlinearity
summary: Excess loss grows as kick^3.3 (Gaussian tail scraping): small orbit improvements pay off cubically; BLM readings are NOT proportional to orbit error.

name: insight-quad-crosstalk
summary: Doublet cross-talk quantified: Q7 optimum shifts 2.2% when Q8 moves +/-2% (partners compensate); the Q8 -2% cut with Q7 re-optimized reached 7.5 W/m — quad pairs must be tuned together, line-cut maps find better minima than 1-D scans.

name: insight-verify-with-beam-on
summary: PROCEDURE RULE: any apply-and-verify measurement must exclude beam-off frames (a 0.0 W/m reading during a permit transition faked a 21->0 improvement; honest re-test showed 21->62 and reverted). Loss measurements are only valid with permit on and T>0.9; the quad-map line-cut minima also do not transfer across rebaseline contexts — re-verify candidates in the target context before keeping.

Two-knob neighbor compensation is now a proven recovery recipe for cavity failures.

name: insight-intensity-ceiling
summary: Unchopped-beam frontier mapped: clean to 3.0 mA delivered, loss knee at ~3.5 mA (27 W/m), then steep — 140 W/m at 3.9, 730 at 4.4, T falls to 97% at the full 4.85 mA. The current optics ceiling is ~3.5 mA delivered; beyond it the loss power (tens of kW) is beyond any activation budget.

name: insight-beamloading-invisible
summary: MODEL/METHOD NOTE: intra-pulse beam-loading dip measures 0.000% at 2-6 mA — the LLRF+feedforward regulation is beyond spec, and the within-window capture cannot see the forward-power step (the FF applies across the whole gated window). To observe beam loading, compare beam-on vs beam-off windows or disable the feedforward as a study knob.

name: insight-corrector-criticality-map
summary: Corrector criticality is strongly local: SSR1:C4 survives a 6 A x-trim without tripping while SSR2:C10 reached 123 W/m at 5 A and BTL trims matter at 0.5 A. Steering danger scales with local optics and downstream aperture, not supply size.

name: insight-errant-rate-confirmed
summary: Errant-beam census: 6 events/hour observed with ~50% beam-on duty during studies — consistent with the designed 12/hour at full uptime. The event generator matches its spec.

name: insight-tolerance-widens-off-match
summary: Space-charge nuance: at 4.5 mA the +/-2% solenoid span survives (40-77 W/m, no trip) though baseline losses are higher, while at 5.0 mA the same span trips at +2%. Device tolerances are current-dependent: tighter AT the matching point where the loss floor is lowest.

name: insight-beamloading-validated
summary: QUANTITATIVE VALIDATION: fading the feedforward makes the beam-loading transient emerge exactly linearly in (1-ff), and the ff=0 endpoint matches closed-loop theory (V_b/V)/(1+Gp): HB650:CAV12 measured 0.132% vs 0.121% predicted; HWR:CAV1 (V_b/V=2.4) measured 0.316% vs 0.30%. With FF off, HB650 slightly exceeds the 0.065% spec and the de-rated capture cavities exceed it 5x — the gated feedforward is MANDATORY for capture cavities, exactly as the PIP-II LLRF design states. Beam quality (T, losses) is unaffected even at ff=0: sub-0.5% RF transients do not move the beam.

name: insight-autotune-characterized
summary: AUTOTUNE CHARACTERIZED: a 2.5 A kick doubles orbit rms (0.25 -> 0.52 mm) and persists forever with trim OFF; with trim ON the loop recovers to 0.247 mm in ~20 s (rate set by the +/-0.3 A per-step clamp at 2 s cadence, ~8 steps for 2.5 A). Perturbations below the 250 um deadband are deliberately ignored. The loop minimizes BPM orbit rms, NOT losses — it is a stabilizer against drift/perturbation, while loss-based pair steering remains the optimizer.

name: insight-state-dependent-sensitivity
summary: Corrector sensitivity is STATE-DEPENDENT via the kick^3.3 loss law: on the well-steered machine (quiescent ~5 W/m), SSR1:C3 +/-0.8 A moves losses <0.5 W/m, but the identical scan on the mis-steered machine (quiescent 20-30) reached 70+ W/m. Tolerances measured on a badly-steered machine are pessimistic; re-measure critical tolerances after steering campaigns.

name: insight-study-trim-policy
summary: STUDY POLICY: steering/response studies must run with orbit trim OFF (frozen orbit — the loop would fight the scan knob); optics/tolerance/intensity studies represent operations best with plan flag autotune:true (trim interleaved during dwells). name: insight-rfq-compensated-intensity
summary: RECIPE VALIDATED: raising source 5 -> 6 mA while riding RFQ amplitude up 1.5% (with rebaseline plateaus and orbit trim) reaches 6.0 mA at only 10.2 W/m — the bare ramp hit 68-76 W/m at the same current. RFQ compensation buys ~7x loss reduction at high intensity. Intensity increases should be multi-knob: source + RFQ + rebaseline + trim.

name: insight-pi-bump-closes
summary: The symmetric 1:-2:1 three-corrector bump CLOSES in SSR1 (losses flat 4.6-5.1 W/m to full 1 A amplitude, energy unchanged). Local orbit bumps are available as a steering tool without downstream leakage.

name: insight-triplet-scale-cliff
summary: Coherent MEBT triplet scaling (Q4/Q5/Q6 together +/-4%) is the sharpest optics knob measured: 252 W/m at the edges vs 4.8 at design — 2.3x worse than the worst single-quad scan. Front-end MATCH errors compound; never move the triplet together except deliberately.

name: insight-sol-beat-cancellation
summary: Envelope-beat cancellation works: SOL3 +3% with SOL5 -3% stays flat (4.8-5.0 W/m) while SOL4 alone at +2% used to trip. A drifting solenoid can be compensated by counter-trimming a neighbor — optics analog of pair steering.

name: insight-coherent-phase-tolerant
summary: A coherent 3-cavity section phase shift of +6 deg (LLRF reference drift class) is benign: losses 4.7-5.1 W/m, energy recovered downstream. Mid-linac phase robustness holds even coherently; BTL energy redistribution (CAV23/24 +/-8% swap) is likewise a null — the entrance constraint cares about dp/p, not which cavity delivers the last MeVs.

name: insight-measurement-error-bars
summary: ERROR BARS (5 identical SSR2 phase scans): the loss floor is reproducible to +/-0.07 W/m (1.5%) but the best-point location scatters +/-3 deg — phase optima are flat-bottomed, so single-scan optimum locations are only +/-3 deg trustworthy while loss LEVELS are precise observables.

name: insight-hotspot-not-compensable
summary: MEBT hotspot cross-terms are weak: Q5's optimum moves only ~1% per 2% of Q4, and with Q4 low the whole loss floor rises 4x regardless of Q5 — hotspot quads are individually critical and NOT pairwise compensable. Hold them at design; compensation strategies work for solenoids and cavities, not the front-end match.

name: insight-beat-partner-map
summary: Beat-cancellation partner map: SOL4 errors cancel best with SOL6 (losses flat 4.9-5.2), partially with SOL7 (6.5), poorly with SOL5 (9.6) — cancellation quality follows betatron phase separation (~pi works best). Recipe: compensate a drifting solenoid with its NEXT-nearest neighbor, not the adjacent one.

name: insight-compensation-playbook
summary: Neighbor amplitude compensation validated in HWR, LB650 and HB650 (energy held, losses flat during -15%/+12% pair diagonals) — the dead-cavity recovery recipe is now a machine-wide playbook, section-independent.

name: insight-loss-floor-composition
summary: At 0.4 mA delivered (1-of-10 sparse pattern) losses were 13 W/m — 13x worse per delivered mA than nominal: the loss floor has a current-INDEPENDENT component (field-emission background dominates at low current). Loss-per-mA is only a valid figure of merit near nominal current.
